entry.title: Out-of-the-Box Document Grounding in SAP AI Core: RAG with SAP Help Portal
entry.content:
Introduction

As enterprises adopt large language models (LLMs), grounding responses in trusted sources is essential to ensure accuracy and contextual relevance. Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) addresses this need by dynamically retrieving relevant content to guide model outputs.
SAP AI Core simplifies this process through its document grounding capability, which allows developers to enhance LLMs with enterprise-specific knowledge. Most notably, 'help.sap.com' is already grounded by default—meaning you can immediately retrieve content from SAP’s official product documentation without any setup. In addition, SAP AI Core supports integration with custom data repositories such as SharePoint, AWS S3, and Metadata, providing flexibility for broader use cases.

This blog focuses on testing RAG retrieval using 'help.sap.com' as a default data repository source, showcasing how easy it is to build a RAG workflow using out-of-the-box SAP Products documentation content. Let’s dive in.

Prerequisite

  • Subscription to SAP AI Core - Extended plan

Restrictions (as of this blog publication):

  • SAP AI Core free tier is only available in SAP BTP free tier, not SAP BTP trial.
  • The Extended Plan for SAP AI Core is only available with CPEA or BTPEA entitlement. You can find more details about pricing and service plans in the Discovery Center here.
  • Not all SAP product documentation is indexed yet within the Help Portal data repository. However, most major SAP solutions are already covered. If you notice occasional gaps or less relevant retrievals, keep in mind that the index is continuously improving over time.

Initial Setup

Step 1: Complete foundational setup of SAP AI Core instance

You can refer to this help documentation that already provide step by step guides here (Enabling the service in Cloud Foundry). Ensure that you choose the extended plan in order to use generative AI capabilities.

Step 2: Download and import Postman collection

To interact with SAP AI Core programmatically—whether for workflow orchestration, document grounding setup, or managing resources—developers can leverage the prebuilt Postman collection available on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

You can refer to this help documentation (Using a 3rd Party API Platform) on the procedure to download JSON collection from SAP Business Accelerator Hub, and import into Postman. In this blog, you will need to download & import these OpenAPI JSON collections:

  • SAP AI Core - AI Core
  • SAP AI Core - Grounding
  • SAP AI Core - Orchestration


Alternatively, you’re free to use your preferred API platform tool—such as Bruno, cURL, or others—based on your workflow and tooling preferences. In this blog, I will be using Postman for demonstration purposes.

Quick Start

REST APIs that we need for this blog are as follows:

  • /SAP AI Core /AI Core /Resource Group > POST /admin/resourceGroups
  • /SAP AI Core /AI Core /Deployment > GET /lm/deployments
  • /SAP AI Core /Grounding /Retrieval > GET /retrieval/dataRepositories
  • /SAP AI Core /Grounding /Retrieval > POST /retrieval/search
  • /SAP AI Core /Orchestration /Completion > POST /completion
Step 1: Create a resource group for document grounding

Before we begin, we a resource group that segment our document grounding workloads. Use the following API endpoint to create a resource group: POST /admin/resourceGroups. Include the following JSON payload in the request body. Make sure to add the label key "ext.ai.sap.com/document-grounding" with the value "true"—this is required to enable document grounding capabilities within the resource group

{
  "resourceGroupId": "<input resource group id here>",
  "labels": [
    {
      "key": "ext.ai.sap.com/document-grounding",
      "value": "true"
    }
  ]
}

In my example, I'm using 'grounding' as my resource group id, but it can be any unique name / id of your choice.

Step 2: List all data repository objects (Optional)

In case you're interested to see all data repository objects that are available in your AI Core resource group, you can use this API: GET /retrieval/dataRepositories

As shown in the response, a default repository named 'help.sap.com' is already provisioned. This built-in integration allows you to perform RAG directly on official SAP Products documentation—enabling immediate access to curated enterprise knowledge without additional setup.

To perform get all data repository objects, remember to update the value of 'AI-Resource-Group' in the request headers. You should see the output below; for my case, I have an additional data repository object from SharePoint, thus another entry showing 'vector' in addition to 'help.sap.com'.



Step 3: Retrieval search via Vector (Approach #1)

There are 2 ways to query grounded documents from data repositories be it from data repository type 'vector' or type 'help.sap.com'. One of the way is using vector retrieval search. This API queries the connected data repository and returns the most relevant content chunks based on the user-provided query: POST /retrieval/search.

To perform retrieval search on 'help.sap.com' data repository, same as above update the value of 'AI-Resource-Group' in the request header, and use this JSON payload in the request body:

{
  "query": "<input your query here>",
  "filters": [
    {
      "id": "filter1",
      "dataRepositoryType": "help.sap.com",
      "dataRepositories": [
        "*"
      ]
    }
  ]
}

In my example, I want to know the official migration guide for SAP PI/PO, thus entering this question "show me migration guide for SAP Process Orchestration" in "query".

Retrieval search results as follows:

As shown above, the response returns grounded content chunks ready for use. To explore the full grounding schema, you can refer to the official documentation.

Step 4: Retrieval search via Orchestration (Approach #2)

This is another way to query grounded documents via orchestration service. What it does it integrates document grounding configurations and filters to provide precise results. With the orchestration service, you can interact with LLMs from multiple providers through a unified interface, without changing your application code. Switching models is as simple as updating a configuration parameter, making it easy to compare performance or adapt to different requirements. It also supports advanced features like prompt templating, content filtering, data masking, and grounding for secure and context-aware AI integration.

To get started, you’ll need to create at least one orchestration deployment—or use the default deployment within your resource group. If no deployments are found, please refer to this tutorial for guidance tutorial.

Otherwise, use GET /lm/deployments to retrieve list of available deployments.

Next, to perform retrieval search using orchestration service with 'help.sap.com' as data repository source, make sure that you update the "deploymentId" URL path variable and update the value of 'AI-Resource-Group' in the request header, then use this example JSON payload in the request body:

{
  "orchestration_config": {
    "module_configurations": {
      "grounding_module_config": {
        "type": "document_grounding_service",
        "config": {
          "filters": [
            {
              "id": "filter1",
              "data_repositories": [
                "*"
              ],
              "search_config": {
                "max_chunk_count": 5
              },
              "data_repository_type": "help.sap.com"
            }
          ],
          "input_params": [
            "groundingRequest"
          ],
          "output_param": "groundingOutput"
        }
      },
      "llm_module_config": {
        "model_name": "<input your llm name, i.e. gpt-35-turbo>",
        "model_params": {},
        "model_version": "<input your llm version, i.e. latest>"
      },
      "templating_module_config": {
        "template": [
          {
            "role": "user",
            "content": "<input your prompt template here, i.e.: Summarize the request using only the provided context.Be concise and accurate.If the context does not contain the answer,say so.Request: {{ ?groundingRequest }} Context: {{ ?groundingOutput }}>"
          }
        ],
        "defaults": {}
      },
      "filtering_module_config": {
        "input": {
          "filters": [
            {
              "type": "azure_content_safety",
              "config": {
                "Hate": 2,
                "SelfHarm": 2,
                "Sexual": 2,
                "Violence": 2
              }
            }
          ]
        },
        "output": {
          "filters": [
            {
              "type": "azure_content_safety",
              "config": {
                "Hate": 2,
                "SelfHarm": 2,
                "Sexual": 2,
                "Violence": 2
              }
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "input_params": {
    "groundingRequest": "<input your search query>"
  }
}

Some quick explanation:
"input_param": Input variables are dynamic parameters provided to the grounding service and referenced in templates to tailor responses based on user input.
"output_param": The output variable stores retrieved content from the grounding service, which is used to generate relevant and informed responses.
"data_repository": You can specify which data repositories to query; if none are selected, all available sources are used by default. In our case, we select all (*).
"template": Templating lets you define prompts with dynamic placeholders (e.g., {{?groundingRequest}}) to customize AI model input during execution.
"llm_model_config": Model configuration allows you to select the generative AI model and set optional parameters in JSON to influence output behavior.

Orchestration service result as follows:

Essentially what I need is the "message" > "content" from "orchestration_result". If you want to explore the full orchestration schema, refer to this official documentation.

Conclusion

As shown above, the retrieval search returns highly relevant results for the given query—including document chunks closely aligned with the intended topic. This significantly reduces the time spent manually searching through documentation, making the process of grounding large language models both efficient and precise. As SAP continues to integrate AI into its business technology ecosystem, capabilities like these will play a key role in delivering intelligent, reliable enterprise applications.

For more information on SAP AI Core

  • Exchange knowledge: SAP Community
  • Learn more: Help Portal | Roadmap | Orchestration
  • Explore: Product Information

Special thanks to product experts that provide me advise during my testing, in particular: @Chai_Wei_Tah @Sumedha_Garg @gesine_stanienda . 
Also do check out other blog from my colleague @rachel_gan  on "Setting up SAP Document Grounding for Chat Apps" with SharePoint as data repository source.

entry.title: Unlocking the Potential of SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath
entry.content:
The rise of artificial intelligence (AI) and robotic process automation (RPA) technologies have empowered companies to leave behind manual, error-prone, and time-consuming work for streamlined operations, customized processes, and end-to-end automated solutions. The most competitive enterprises today are developing a lasting edge by taking advantage of these capabilities.

To give our customers an even greater edge with their automation toolbox, in Q4 2024 we released SAP’s Solution Extension with UiPath to integrate with their leading RPA to automate non-SAP environments.

With the new SAP Build Process Automations integrated with UiPath, customers can extend the entire SAP enterprise automation framework, whether they be between SAP and non-SAP applications, on-premises and multi-cloud environments, and systems that span customers, employees and partners.

 

How UiPath extends the value of enterprise automation with SAP
Enterprise automation from SAP uses best-in-class SAP technologies – SAP Integration Suite, SAP Signavio solutions, AI Agents and now SAP Build integrated with UiPath – to help connect and automate complex, end-to-end business processes. These technologies work in concert to enable customers to quickly and easily connect any on-premises or cloud IT landscape, including SAP and non-SAP systems, to gain visibility into business processes and their performance, and then automate those processes seamlessly.

With this partnership, it provides SAP customers flexibility to maintain their UiPath investments while leveraging a single process orchestration layer for both SAP Build Process Automation and UiPath robots to automate non-SAP processes.  As for automating SAP processes, we continue to invest in SAP Build Process Automation and its extensive pre-build workflows and automations for SAP applications. It also serves as the foundation for Joule Studio in SAP Build that customers can use to build AI agents.

 

Fast track cloud migration with proven accelerators
The move from customized ERP systems to S/4HANA Cloud is time consuming and risky while ensuring data integrity during the complex migration process.  By leveraging SAP Activate methodology and SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath robots with proven migration content, data is accurately extracted by identifying complex code, analyze vulnerabilities, check for compatibility with S/4 and remediate any issues automatically to simplify migration efforts and prevents manual errors.

Continuous process improvement to surpass competition
Organizations face continuous changes in processes, applications and industry best practices, which can break automated strategic end-to-end business processes.  To maintain process transparency, enterprise automation with SAP portfolio includes integrated triggers that recognize inefficiencies or events, and to adjust the predetermined behavior of automation methodologies for greater productivity in S/4HANA.  To minimize disruption from application changes, SAP iPaaS and RPA can automate core processes and utilize automation techniques.  As for best practices to remain competitive, SAP is the process leader across 25 industries where knowledge is captured in industry proven benchmark and pre-built content delivered through SAP Business Accelerator hub that can be rapidly deployed.


Automate enterprise processes to optimize your business
Automating strategic end-to-end processes often require a variety of automation technologies (RPA, workflow, systems integration, APIs and events powered by AI) across heterogenous landscapes.  Enterprise automation with SAP portfolio is a Gartner market leader with profiling, iPaaS, and now RPA, which provides the process orchestration layer for comprehensive interoperability across all these automation methods.

SAP and UiPath Together: One Platform, Limitless Potential
By integrating SAP Build Process Automation workflows with UiPath’s RPA and third-party connectors, we’re offering our customers one of the largest libraries of pre-built content and accelerators to help address their comprehensive processes that span multiple applications within a complex IT landscape—regardless of the cloud software provider.  To illustrate the power of SAP and UiPath synergy, let’s examine a business scenario that shows real life automations across SAP and non-SAP environments as well as other highlights across the integrated solution.

Procurement: MRP-to-goods receipt journey
Manual interventions in the source-to-pay journey can weigh down business operations.  When processes traverse many different applications, tracking down the process exceptions or even the status of any process instance can be a laborious task requiring highly specialized skills.  Let’s take a look as to how SAP and UiPath can work together to deliver a seamless procurement journey.

The process starts with an MRP run in a manufacturing operations management solution, like Siemens Opcenter

  1. The creation of the purchase requisitions trigger the SAP Build Process Automation add-on by UiPath to create these requisitions in SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing
  2. Once in Ariba, the created requisitions are routed to the correct approver based on the SAP Build Process Automation business rules engine
  3. Approved requisitions are converted to purchase orders in Ariba and Oracle WMS is updated with the PO data for goods receipt
  4. Finally, SAP S/4HANA Cloud is updated with all relevant data to ensure prompt and accurate invoice processing.

As you can see, this process is relatively complex, yet the power of SAP and UiPath enable seamless automation across the many systems as well as a central monitoring view to be able to quickly respond to any processing exceptions.

 

Process improvement
To gain a comprehensive view of your processes across your ERP landscape, you can pinpoint bottlenecks and uncover areas with the greatest automation potential. More importantly, with contextual best practices and prebuilt content for SAP S/4HANA Cloud and other cloud solutions from SAP, your organization can receive actionable recommendations to refine its processes. For example, inefficiencies identified in SAP Signavio solutions can trigger smart actions in SAP Build—turning insights into impactful automations.

 

Enterprise-grade governance
Combine SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath’s capabilities and the comprehensive security features of SAP Business Technology Platform to help ensure compliance and trust in enterprise-wide automation efforts. 

 

Orchestrated task automation
Coordinate workflows with smooth connectivity to the SAP Task Center service, driving consistent and optimized operations.


Combined development tools
SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath combines workflow management, robotic process automation (RPA), and embedded AI into a unified low-code/no-code environment. Designed for business users and IT teams, the Automation designs originate as projects within SAP Build Process Automation, serving as the foundation for streamlined workflows and advanced process orchestration.

Key features include:

  • Integrated development tools to build business rules, approval workflows, RPA bots, and generative AI models
  • Comprehensive workflow management to orchestrate processes, monitor tasks in real time, and gain actionable insights.  Customizable process extensions help keep the ERP core clean while extending functionality with drag-and-drop tools and embedded security guardrails
  • Native integrations with SAP Integration Suite, SAP S/4HANA Cloud, SAP Signavio solutions, and other SAP software for a unified experience
  • Cost efficiency through native embedding
    Decrease integration and maintenance costs by embedding UiPath RPA directly into SAP Build Process Automation. Unlike standalone RPA solutions, SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath eliminate the need for extensive third-party integrations.
  • Advanced monitoring for real-time insights
    This integration features authentication enabled by SAP Cloud Identity Services that gives access to UiPath services, while lifecycle synchronization helps ensure automations remain aligned across both solutions. UiPath monitoring is also fully integrated into SAP Build Process Automation, providing unified tracking and enhanced visibility into automation processes.

 

  • Digital access license is not required to connect with SAP systems
  • Streamlined design and deployment
    Use drag-and-drop tools to efficiently build and deploy automation workflows. This capability simplifies the design process and accelerates automation implementation.  For example, workflows and processes can be streamlined by leverage bots directly within SAP Build Process Automation.

    •  

Advanced automation tools

  • Comprehensive automation at scale beyond SAP solutions: Launch automation projects with thousands of prebuilt templates for third-party systems to create a unified enterprise automation ecosystem across the business.
  • AI-driven automation for smarter workflows:  Leverage built-in AI capabilities, such as intelligent document processing and predictive analytics, to enhance the efficiency of automations. Unlike many standalone RPA solutions, the SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath combines AI and automation to handle complex, unstructured data and document processing into workflows.
  • Unified automation for complex processes:  Empower your business by automating entire workflows, helping ensure comprehensive process optimization across multiple systems and reducing fragmentation in automation efforts.
  • Cloud-based bot execution:  Transition to the cloud with bots to connect with SAP solutions securely and efficiently through remote calls and surface automation using screen recordings and UI-element captures.  This connectivity supports a range of SAP user interface technologies, including SAP Fiori apps, SAPUI5, GUI, WebGUI, and line-of-business software, such as SAP S/4HANA, SAP SuccessFactors solutions, and third-party applications. With SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath, these options provide prebuilt content, native connectors, and superior surface automation for a seamless integration experience.
  • Process optimization with advanced insights:  Leverage task mining and live insights into SAP Signavio solutions, so you can uncover inefficiencies and transform processes with actionable data.

 

How UiPath strengthens Enterprise Automation with SAP
With more than 50 years of expertise in mission-critical business processes, SAP continues to lead the way in enterprise automation.  By combining trusted solutions with the latest technologies, SAP empowers organizations to simplify operations, drive innovation, and scale transformation.

SAP Build Process Automation with UiPath are part of that long-standing legacy. By offering a clear path to unify, optimize, and future-proof your processes, the integration not only elevate automation strategies but also unlock new levels of resilience and growth.

Ready to see how enterprise automation can transform your business? Interact with our demo of SAP Build Process Automation add-on by UiPath.

 

To learn more, please check out our upcoming UiPath Solution Extension Blog Series from our SAP Product Engineering Owner, Deval Khatri, @devalk

entry.title: New Basic Trial available: Get your hands on Enterprise Automation with SAP
entry.content:
In today’s fast-paced business landscape, automation has emerged as a crucial competitive differentiator. While most stakeholders understand its strategic value and potential benefits, many organizations still face significant challenges on the path to becoming truly autonomous.

Enterprise Automation with SAP is the only solution that offers a comprehensive, integrated suite of tools for process mining, automation, integration, monitoring, and continuous optimization of workflows. It empowers businesses to streamline operations and drive intelligent decision-making—without adding unnecessary complexity.

 

 

With SAP Signavio, SAP Build, and SAP Integration Suite, customers gain access to best-in-class capabilities. These tools work in harmony, enabling users to quickly uncover insights, automate processes, and integrate seamlessly with existing applications to deliver the right outcomes.

Now, there’s a powerful new way to explore the full potential of Enterprise Automation with SAP. The new Basic Trial gives you hands-on access to SAP Build, SAP Integration Suite, and SAP Signavio—all in one end-to-end scenario. It’s the perfect opportunity to experience the value of automation in action. In order to activate your basic trial, you need to navigate to the Enterprise Automation webpage on SAP.com:

Access to Basic Trial for Enterprise Automation

As the next step you need to click on the ,,Start your free 30-day trial" button that will forward you to the next page.

 

 

You are now being forwarded to the Basic Trial page of SAP Build Process Automation, here you simply click on ,,Try now". This process will generate log-in credentials which you can use right away to start your hands-on tutorial.

 

 

After logging in you will be redirected to the tutorial landing page. As a last step you need to select the Enterprise Automation workbook and start your tutorial:

 

 

This includes a 2 hour hands-on exercise with 5 different units, which you can discover at your own pace:

 

 

If you want to learn more about the Basic Trial and the use case, that is being covered in this hands-on feel free to listen to our webcast, which you can find here.

 

Happy hacking!

entry.title: Sustainability Control Tower: Update on SAP-provided Metrics on ESRS regulation
entry.content:
Co-author @elsandoval 

Disclaimer: This blog post reflects the state of the SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT) as of April 2025. 

SAP Sustainability Control Tower (SCT) supports customers on ESG reporting. Therefore, SCT enhances the coverage of metrics provided which are in line with the European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS). 

This blog-post provides an overview of metrics which are planned or newly released and gets updated regularly. For the full list of available metrics in SCT, please visit Metrics | SAP Help Portal 

 

Planned Delivery for May (as of April 16th 2025)

Qualitative

Sustainability MatterMetric NameDisclosure RequirementData Point
CLIMATE CHANGEProcess of Climate-Related Considerations in Executive Bodies Remuneration E1.GOV-3 (13) (1)
CLIMATE CHANGEExposure and Sensitivity Assessment: Climate-Related Hazards with Gross Physical RiskE1.IRO-1(20bii)(AR 11, AR 13-AR 15)
CLIMATE CHANGEExposure Assessment: Climate-Transition Events with Gross Transition RiskE1.IRO-1(20cii)(AR 11-AR 15)
CLIMATE CHANGEClimate Change Mitigation Transition Plan E1-1(14)(AR 1)
CLIMATE CHANGEAlignment of Targets to Paris AgreementE1-1(16a)(AR 2)
CLIMATE CHANGEDecarbonization Levers and Actions E1-1(16b)
CLIMATE CHANGELocked-In GHG Emissions and Risks in GHG Emission Reduction TargetsE1-1(16d)(AR 3)
CLIMATE CHANGEEconomy Activities Alignment Plans with Criteria in R2139E1-1(16e)(AR 4)
CLIMATE CHANGETransition Plan Integration with Strategy and FinanceE1-1(16h)
CLIMATE CHANGEIs Transition Plan by Executives BodiesE1-1(16i)

CLIMATE CHANGEProcess of Assessment on Climate Transition Risks and OpportunitiesE1-1(20c)(AR 13-AR 14)
CLIMATE CHANGEProcess of Identification of Transition Events Over TimeE1-1(AR 12a, AR 13-AR 14) (1)
CLIMATE CHANGEScreening for Exposure to Transition EventsE1-1(AR 12a, AR 13-AR 14) (2)
CLIMATE CHANGEAssets and Operations Exposed to Transition EventsE1-1(AR 12b, AR 13-AR 14)

entry.title: Streamlining User Management: Integrating SAP Integration Suite with SAP Cloud Identity Services (3)
entry.content:
Streamlining User Management: Integrating SAP Integration Suite with SAP Cloud Identity Services - Part 3

In the evolving landscape of SAP environments, effective user management is critical for maintaining operational efficiency and security. Our previous discussions delved into the fusion of SAP Integration Suite with SAP Cloud Identity Services, covering essentials like user group setup, role collection mappings and Two-Factor Authentication (2FA). As we advance in this series, the upcoming article will provide key recommendations to further customize and refine this foundational setup to meet the dynamic demands of modern enterprises.

Setup Instructions

Filters for groups / users

Properties hold the configuration of a provisioning system. Based on the presented scenario might make sense to setup individual filter settings. The following sections will highlight settings that might be useful to you setup.

I.Configure: ias.user.filter for the Identity Authentication source system. This setting offers the ability to filter upon your requirements e.g. exclude platform provisioning user

i. Navigate to the Properties tab of the Identity Authentication source system
ii. Create standard property of type ias.user.filter
iii. Set exclusion rule e.g. userName ne "cf-user-provisioning-<instance>-platform@sap.invalid"

II. Configure: ias.group.filter for the Identity Authentication source system. The option enables to filter groups upon custom requirements e.g. provision limited set of groups to target system.

i. Navigate to the Properties tab of the Identity Authentication source system
ii. Create standard property of type ias.group.filter
iii. Define rule e.g. displayName sw "BTP_"

SAP Reference: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/list-of-properties 

Job scheduling

You can execute a provisioning job through API requests.

I. You must have a technical user with the Access Identity Provisioning Tenant Admin API permission granted.
II. Identify the source system id of choice

i.  Navigate to the source system ii. The source system id is accessible via the following URI

III. Query Identity Provisioning API for test purposes with JobType READ. Use your tool of preference e.g. curlV. Setup recurring API call with JobType RESYNC instead of READ via SAP Job Scheduling Service. Detailed instructions on how to setup jobs using the service is explained by my colleague @CarlosRoggan in the blog series "Job Scheduling Service in SAP Business Technology Platform: Overview of Blog Posts".

SAP Reference: https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/run-provisioning-jobs-via-api, https://help.sap.com/docs/identity-authentication/identity-authentication/managing-administrators?version=Cloud, https://api.sap.com/api/IPS_Proxy/overview, https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/serviceCatalog/job-scheduling-service?region=all

Remove "Sign in with Default Identity Provider" option from SAP BTP XSUAA page

I. Open the BTP cockpit
II. Navigate to your subaccount
III. Go to Security" -> "Trust Configuration
IV. Click the Pencil icon under Default or Default Identity Provider
V. Deselect the Available for User Logon option.

SAP Reference: https://me.sap.com/notes/0003355200 

Outlook

In today’s fast-paced digital world, efficient user management is crucial for any enterprise utilizing SAP systems. SAP Cloud Identity Services offer a comprehensive solution designed to optimize and streamline these processes, addressing critical issues such as inefficiencies, security vulnerabilities, and compliance challenges inherent in traditional user management methods. By leveraging SAP Cloud Identity Services, organizations can simplify access control through role-based provisioning, enhance security with multi-factor authentication, and reduce administrative burdens through automation and centralized user management.

Implementing SAP Cloud Identity Services allows businesses to transition to a more agile and secure cloud-based environment. With its seamless integration capabilities, the service centralizes user management, significantly boosting operational efficiency and safeguarding sensitive data. Organizations benefit through measurable outcomes like reduced onboarding times and improved compliance, evidenced by numerous success stories and case studies within the SAP community.

Looking ahead, colleague @desiree_degutis  will write a subsequent article focusing on automating the user onboarding process. This future post will delve into custom automation solutions that capitalize on the efficiencies gained from SAP Cloud Identity Services, promising to further enhance user management practices and streamline operations.

By embracing SAP Cloud Identity Services now, and preparing for automation strategies discussed in upcoming article, SAP community members can stay ahead in the digital landscape, ensuring robust and efficient user management. Stay tuned for more insights and strategies in this evolving space.

entry.title: Lets Talk to the Database - CAP & SQLite Powered by Google Gemini-2.5-Pro
entry.content:
I am sure everyone is doing great !!!

I am glad and excited to share my learning on Talk to Database , I mean just talk to the database using Natural Language powered by Google Gemini-2.5-pro-exp handled by SAP CAP programming.

So what all we have here to achieve this end-to-end ?

  • We have access to Gemini-2.5-pro exprimetnal version available ( PAID ) Get your API Keys to interact through API Call
  • A CAP Application where we have deployed Sales Order header / Line Item / Deliveries Entities Deployed
  • Install SQLITE DB For Data Storage for all the 3 Tables
  • Add some Dummy data into your DB tables So that later we can validate data and interact with the database for fetching requested information and run select statement on top of that.

Test out Gemini API using POSTMAN before you jump into this.

Data Prep:

  • Extracted data from Sales Order Header (VBAK)
  • Extracted data from Sales Line Item (VBAP)
  • Extracted data from Delivery (LIPS)

 Tasks Break Down:

Prompt Prep :

  1. Prepare the prompt and provide enough context to generate SQL
  2. Example  Prompt – Give me number of Sales Order in System
  3. Prompt Specifies --  A Count is required from Sales Order Header table

Context - 

  1. Sales Order Header = SalesOrderH Table
  2. Sales Order Item = SalesOrderItem

Instruction –

`You are a specialized SQL query assistant for a computer store database. Your primary goal is to answer user questions by retrieving data using the available tools.

Database Context:

The key tables you will interact with are:

   'sp_prompts_Delivery': Contains Delivery Data.

   'sp_prompts_SalesOrderHeader': Contains Sales Order Header Data (often referred to as just Sales Orders).

   'sp_prompts_SalesOrderItem': Contains detailed Sales Order Item Data. `

 

Identify Tables :

  1. Develop NodeJS API to extract tables names Specified
  2. Develop NodeJS API to list all the tables:

Example  - http://localhost:4004/odata/v4/my/listTables

     

 Benefit – this will help in finding out extract matching table while building the select query , will help in preparing the right context for model input

Schema Extraction : Develop Nodejs API to extract schema of tables ( Entities ) specified

Extract Schema for any give table input

Example –

http://localhost:4004/odata/v4/my/describeDbTable

Content-Type: application/json

{   "tableName":"sp_prompts_SalesOrderHeader"  }

Benefit: this will help in preparing schema for tables incase we need only few columns or specific column like Sales Order Types / Delivery Types etc.

 

Execute DB Query:  This is the most important one once Model has generated the select statement for the input , this will execute the query and provide the response

Example – Prompt - SELECT * FROM sp_prompts_SalesOrderItem

Response :

Benefit: - It will run the DB Statements and will return the results

 

Prepare Response:

This will come into picture in case we are requesting some information and expect the response in a plain english Language

Example – Give me no of Sales Order and Sales Item from System

If you notice the generated sql then you will get the idea what happening behind the scene.

Generated SQL

CAP Structure:

This is how my CAP Structure look like.

QueryEngine Contains the Main API Call to Gemini which subsequently calls all the functions required to get data back from Database like below.

AI Model Used :  Gemini-2.5-pro-exp

Initialize of GenAI Model like below.

If you see we have passed set of instructions that contains information about the database and tools are nothing the list of function which will give information about Tables and their Schema and later the final function for executing the generated query.

Dbquery.cds

This action will be responsible for prompt handing and it will return object of Information.

Dbquery.JS

This will handle all the incoming requests ---QueryDatabase is the one which will be triggered from outside rest of functions will be called internally , these are listed for standalone testing.

Note

I am also attaching a small Video Clip where you can see the execution of various prompts for technical implementation DM me directly , i can help you in building this POC.

#KeepLearningKeepSharing

entry.title: Document Grounding in SAP AI Core: RAG with AWS S3 via Object Store Service on SAP BTP
entry.content:
Introduction

In my previous post, I showed how to get started with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) using SAP AI Core’s built-in integration, leveraging out-of-the-box SAP product documentation as the default grounding content—no setup required. But what if you want to ground your LLMs using your own documents?

In this follow-up, I’ll show you how to configure AWS S3 (via the Object Store service on SAP BTP) as a custom data repository source for document grounding. This gives you the flexibility to serve proprietary or domain-specific content into your RAG workflow—all while using the same orchestration and retrieval APIs in SAP AI Core.

If you want to the configuration with Microsoft SharePoint, you can refer to another blog from my colleague @rachel_gan  on "Setting up SAP Document Grounding for Chat Apps" or developer tutorial "Orchestration with Grounding Capabilities in SAP AI Core".

Let’s walk through the setup.

Prerequisite

  • Subscription to Object Store on SAP BTP - Standard plan (AWS regions)
  • Subscription to SAP AI Core - Extended plan
  • Subscription to SAP AI Launchpad – Recommended for easier management, and testing (this blog will demonstrate API-based steps with Postman)

Restrictions (as of this blog publication):

  • Object Store on SAP BTP is only available with CPEA or BTPEA entitlement. You can find more details about pricing and service plans in the Discovery Center here.
  • SAP AI Core free tier is only available in SAP BTP free tier, not SAP BTP trial.
  • The Extended Plan for SAP AI Core is only available with CPEA or BTPEA entitlement. You can find more details about pricing and service plans in the Discovery Center here.

Initial Setup

Step 1: Service instance & Service key creation

This guide assumes you're already familiar with subscribing to SAP BTP services via the Service Marketplace, creating service instances, and generating service keys.
You should have the following already set up:

  • SAP Object Store (in an AWS region) – Ensure you’ve provisioned the service and obtained the service key. You can follow this cloud foundry command line guide (need only from steps 1 to 5) for reference.
  • SAP AI Core (Extended Plan) – A foundational instance should be in place. If not, refer to the official setup documentation under “Enabling the Service in Cloud Foundry”. Be sure to select the extended plan to access generative AI features.
Step 2: Download and import Postman collection

For the SAP Object Store on BTP (AWS), the service provides native AWS S3 buckets as object storage resources. This means there’s no custom API variation—you can interact with the buckets using standard AWS S3 REST APIs and authenticate using the AWS Signature mechanism.
Use the values provided in your Object Store service key to complete the mapping.

Example of REST API that you will be using:
GET - List available objects

PUT - Upload a file object

For SAP AI Core, you can refer to my previous blog post - same section.
Alternatively, you’re free to use your preferred API platform tool—such as Bruno, cURL, or others—based on your workflow and tooling preferences. In this blog, I will be using Postman for demonstration purposes.

Quick Start

REST APIs that we need for this blog are as follows:

  • /Object Store > PUT - Upload a file object
  • /Object Store > GET- List available objects
  • /SAP AI Core /AI Core /Secret > POST /admin/secrets
  • /SAP AI Core /Grounding /Retrieval > GET /retrieval/dataRepositories
  • /SAP AI Core /Grounding /Retrieval > POST /retrieval/search
  • /SAP AI Core /Orchestration /Completion > POST /completion
Step 1: Create a resource group for document grounding

Before we begin, we need a dedicated resource group to organize and manage our document grounding workloads. If you haven’t already created one, please refer to my previous blog post for creating a resource group for document grounding.

Step 2: Create a generic secret

Create a new generic secret at the resource group level. This secret is required to allow the document grounding process to access your Object Store - AWS S3 bucket.
Update the 'AI-Resource-Group' value in the request headers, and include the following JSON payload in the request body. Make sure to add the label key "ext.ai.sap.com/document-grounding" with the value "true", and the label key "ext.ai.sap.com/documentRepositoryType" with the value "S3". These labels are required to enable document grounding capabilities and specify AWS S3 as the repository source for your resource group.

In my example below, I named my generic secret as 'object-store-secret', you can use any unique name of your choice.

{
  "name": "<input unique generic secret name, i.e. object-store-secret>",
  "data": {
    "url": "<Input Base64 encoded value in the format https://s3-<region>.amazonaws.com>",
    "description": "<Input Base64 encoded value for the description of the generic secret to be created>", 
    "authentication": "<Input Base64 encoded value for NoAuthentication, i.e. Tm9BdXRoZW50aWNhdGlvbg==>", 
    "access_key_id": "<Input Base64 encoded value for access key id>",                 
    "bucket": "<Input Base64 encoded value for AWS S3 bucket name>",                               
    "host": "<Input Base64 encoded value for AWS S3 host>",                                    
    "region": "<Input Base64 encoded value for AWS S3 region>",
    "username": "<Input Base64 encoded value for AWS S3 credentials>",
    "secret_access_key": "<Input Base64 encoded value for AWS S3 credentials>"                      
  },
  "labels": [
    {
      "key": "ext.ai.sap.com/document-grounding",
      "value": "true"
    },
    {
      "key": "ext.ai.sap.com/documentRepositoryType",
      "value": "S3"
    }
  ]
}

Step 3: Upload a PDF document into Object Store - AWS S3 bucket

Use the standard AWS S3 API (e.g., PUT Object) to upload your PDF file to the designated bucket.
Note: You don't need to include /BTP/ in the file path—it's optional. Just make sure your filename includes the correct file extension (e.g., .pdf).
In my example, I’m using a PDF that outlines SAP Joule capabilities as the basis for RAG. If you’d like to follow along using the same document, you can download it here.

Step 4: List all objects in Object Store - AWS S3 bucket (Optional)

To verify the upload or browse contents, use the ListObjects API to retrieve a list of all files stored in the bucket. This helps confirm that your document is ready for grounding.

Step 5: Create a pipeline

This step is essential, as the pipeline defines how content is processed and grounded from your AWS S3 bucket. Also remember to update the value of 'AI-Resource-Group' in the request headers, and use this JSON payload in the request body:

{
  "type": "S3",
  "configuration": {
    "destination": "<enter generic secret name i.e. object-store-secret>"
  }
}

Once the pipeline is successfully created and triggered, the uploaded PDF will be processed—its content converted into vector embeddings for retrieval. You can verify that the grounding process completed by checking the pipeline execution status.

Step 6: List all data repository objects (Optional)

If you’d like to view all data repository objects available in your SAP AI Core resource group, use the following API: GET /retrieval/dataRepositories
As shown in the response, a new data repository named 'S3' is already provisioned. This built-in integration enables you to run RAG workflows directly on your AWS S3 bucket—providing access to enterprise content without extra configuration. Make sure to set the 'AI-Resource-Group' value in the request headers before making the call. You should see a response similar to the one below.

Step 7: Retrieval search via Vector (Approach #1)

There are two ways to query grounded documents from data repositories—whether the type is 'help.sap.com' or 'vector'. In this scenario, we’ll be working with a 'vector' data repository.
One approach is to use vector retrieval search, which allows you to query the connected repository and receive the most relevant content chunks based on a user-defined query. To do this, use the following API: POST /retrieval/search.

As with previous requests, make sure to set the 'AI-Resource-Group' value in the request headers, and include the following JSON payload in the request body:

{
  "query": "<input your query here>",
  "filters": [
    {
      "id": "filter1",
      "dataRepositoryType": "vector",
      "dataRepositories": [
        "*"
      ]
    }
  ]
}

In below example, I’m interested in learning about the capabilities of SAP Joule, so I’ve entered the query: "What are the use cases supported in Joule?" You can also enhance the search with configuration options—for instance, setting "maxChunkCount": 10 to limit the number of returned chunks to 10.

Retrieval search results as follows:

As shown above, the response returns grounded content chunks ready for use. To explore the full grounding schema, you can refer to the official documentation.

Step 8: Retrieval search via Orchestration (Approach #2)

This is another way to query grounded documents via orchestration service. What it does it integrates document grounding configurations and filters to provide precise results.
With the orchestration service, you can interact with LLMs from multiple providers through a unified interface, without changing your application code. Switching models is as simple as updating a configuration parameter, making it easy to compare performance or adapt to different requirements. It also supports advanced features like prompt templating, content filtering, data masking, and grounding for secure and context-aware AI integration.

To get started, you’ll need to create at least one orchestration deployment—or use the default deployment within your resource group. If no deployments are found, please refer to this tutorial for guidance tutorial. Otherwise, use GET /lm/deployments to retrieve list of available deployments.

Next, to perform retrieval search using orchestration service with 'vector' as data repository source, make sure that you update the "deploymentId" URL path variable and update the value of 'AI-Resource-Group' in the request header, then use this example JSON payload in the request body:

{
  "orchestration_config": {
    "module_configurations": {
      "grounding_module_config": {
        "type": "document_grounding_service",
        "config": {
          "filters": [
            {
              "id": "filter1",
              "data_repositories": [
                "*"
              ],
              "search_config": {
                "max_chunk_count": 10
              },
              "data_repository_type": "vector"
            }
          ],
          "input_params": [
            "groundingRequest"
          ],
          "output_param": "groundingOutput"
        }
      },
      "llm_module_config": {
        "model_name": "<input your llm name, i.e. gpt-35-turbo>",
        "model_params": {},
        "model_version": "<input your llm version, i.e. latest>"
      },
      "templating_module_config": {
        "template": [
          {
            "role": "user",
            "content": "<input your prompt template here, i.e.: Summarize the request using only the provided context.Be concise and accurate.If the context does not contain the answer,say so.Request: {{ ?groundingRequest }} Context: {{ ?groundingOutput }}>"
          }
        ],
        "defaults": {}
      },
      "filtering_module_config": {
        "input": {
          "filters": [
            {
              "type": "azure_content_safety",
              "config": {
                "Hate": 2,
                "SelfHarm": 2,
                "Sexual": 2,
                "Violence": 2
              }
            }
          ]
        },
        "output": {
          "filters": [
            {
              "type": "azure_content_safety",
              "config": {
                "Hate": 2,
                "SelfHarm": 2,
                "Sexual": 2,
                "Violence": 2
              }
            }
          ]
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "input_params": {
    "groundingRequest": "<input your search query>"
  }
}

Some quick explanation:
"input_param": Input variables are dynamic parameters provided to the grounding service and referenced in templates to tailor responses based on user input.
"output_param": The output variable stores retrieved content from the grounding service, which is used to generate relevant and informed responses. "data_repository": You can specify which data repositories to query; if none are selected, all available sources are used by default. In our case, we select all (*).
"template": Templating lets you define prompts with dynamic placeholders (e.g., {{?groundingRequest}}) to customize AI model input during execution.
"llm_model_config": Model configuration allows you to select the generative AI model and set optional parameters in JSON to influence output behavior.

Orchestration service result as follows:

Essentially what I need is the "message" > "content" from "orchestration_result". If you want to explore the full orchestration schema, refer to this official documentation.

And this is how it looked like using SAP AI Launchpad (please excuse below low-res gif):

Conclusion

With SAP AI Core’s support for document grounding via AWS S3—enabled through the Object Store service on SAP BTP—you can bring your own enterprise content into Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) workflows with minimal setup. This blog walked through the key steps: setting up the Object Store, uploading documents, configuring access, and performing retrieval searches.
By grounding LLMs with your domain-specific content, you enhance the accuracy and trustworthiness of AI responses—laying the foundation for smarter, more context-aware enterprise applications.

For more information on SAP AI Core

  • Exchange knowledge: SAP Community
  • Learn more: Help Portal | Roadmap | Orchestration
  • Explore: Product Information

entry.title: Beyond SAP Cloud - Episode 18: BTP Sub Account for administration
entry.content:
In this session, we explain the importance of having a separate administration sub account in BTP for managing reusable services like transport management, Automation Pilot, and CI/CD. This approach ensures security by isolating environments (development, acceptance, production) and preventing unnecessary access to sensitive data. The administration sub account allows for centralized management, reusability of services across sub accounts, and clear segregation of duties, making it a crucial aspect for organizations to maintain control over their development and production processes.

Takeaways

  • A separate administration sub account is essential for managing reusable services across multiple environments.
  • Transport management services should be isolated in the administration sub account to prevent unauthorized access to production or acceptance environments.
  • Placing sensitive services like transport management in development accounts can lead to security risks, as developers could unintentionally access critical environments.
  • The administration sub account enables the segregation of duties, ensuring clear responsibilities for administration, development, and security.
  • Reusable services like the automation pilot can be centralized in the administration sub account for efficiency and easier management.
  • CI/CD services can either be centrally located in the administration sub account or placed within each development sub account based on specific workflows.
  • Developers should not have access to production or acceptance environments in their development accounts to prevent unwanted data or system exposure.
  • The administration sub account is crucial for ensuring reusability of services between different sub accounts, supporting scalability.
  • Automation pilot services are designed to streamline administrative tasks, making it a good candidate for the administration sub account.
  • The decision of where to place CI/CD services depends on the needs of the development team, particularly how the build code interacts with the CI/CD system.
  • Implementing a clear separation between different environments (development, acceptance, production) helps maintain security and minimizes potential risks in system access.

entry.title: New Machine Learning and AI features in SAP HANA Cloud 2025 Q1
entry.content:
With the SAP HANA Cloud 2025 Q1 release, several new embedded Machine Learning / AI functions have been released with the SAP HANA Cloud Predictive Analysis Library (PAL). Key new capabilities to be highlighted are introduction of text analysis, text vectorization, vector data as input to many more Machine Learning functions like classification and regression in the SAP HANA Cloud database. An enhancement summary is available in the What’s new document for SAP HANA Cloud database 2025.02 (QRC 1/2025).

Text extraction and text search enhancements

Text extraction from binary files

A new Document Filter Library (DFL) has been released, which provides functions to extract plain text from binary files like PDFs stored as BLOBs in SAP HANA Cloud tables. By use of the DFL_EXTRACT_TEXT_FROM_DOCUMENTS procedure, application developers and data scientist are enabled to unlock the valuable insights from text hidden in those files.

Extracted text is made available for text preprocessing tasks like chunking, NLP (e.g. entity extraction, sentiment detection), text vectorization using SAP HANA Cloud embedding models and further downstream analysis tasks based on text vectors such as similarity search, text mining, clustering and classification.

This enhancement not only widens the scope of your data analysis but also makes it easier to leverage text-based insights in your applications.

The Document Filter Library (DFL) is provided as a SAP HANA Cloud plugin. The setup instructions are documented in the SAP HANA Cloud administration guide and include the following steps

  • the plugin needs to be installed and Script Server activated
  • SQL user privileges need to be granted

Further note, the document filter library in SAP HANA Cloud provides comparable text from binary files extraction-capabilities like the text analysis and full text indexing from binary files features in SAP HANA Platform, for respective reference see the text analysis SQL API for extraction of text from binary documents in the Text Analysis Developer Guide or the SAP HANA search developer guide for document mime-type reference.

 

Keyword-based text search enhancements

Information retrieval from text typically starts with text search techniques. The newly released keyword-based search function BM25 search (SEARCH_DOCS_BY_KEYWORDS) has been further enhanced to support complete natural sentences as input to the BM25 search:

 

Classification, regression, time series and AutoML model enhancements

Hybrid Gradient Boosting Trees (HGBT) enhancements

HGBT now supports multi-target regression and multi-label classification models, alike MT_MLP modelling predictions for multiple columns using a single model.

  • Multiple DEPENDENT_VARIABLE values can be set as parameters to enable this
  • In addition, REAL_VECTOR columns are supported as target, and a multiple target regression model applied.
  • The objective functions supported for multi-target/label models
    • Regression OBJ_FUNC = 0 (Squared error)
    • Classification OBJ_FUNC = 7 (Softmax)
  • Using the new parameter USE_VEC_LEAF, allows to control for each tree leaf to have a vector-value as a predictor (default is no)

As the prediction output incl. values for multiple targets or classes, a new function  PAL_HGBT_MULTI_TASK_PREDICT is introduced generating the extended output

 

HGBT regression models with trend extrapolation for Time Series

Tree-based models like Hybrid Gradient Boosting Trees are great at capturing patterns from the training data. However, they falter when it comes to projecting these patterns into the future, hence especially when models like HGBT regressors are applied in time series forecasting. A regular such model has no means to infer trends or patterns beyond the bounds of the training data, making true extrapolation impossible. For a detailed discussion see for example this blog post: overcoming the limitations of tree-based models in time series forecasting.

HGBTs, now introduces a linear component to the tree-building process, allowing the model to capture linear patterns and thus trend extrapolation capabilities

  • New parameter MODEL_TREE, when setting it to linear, the model will try to fit a linear model at each leaf node.

With this key enhancement PAL Hybrid Gradient Boosting Tree (HGBT) now allows for building superior regression models, with improved trend extrapolation of value outside of the training data scope. 

 

Unified classification / regression enhancements

The modernized and much enhanced Multi-task MLP Neural Network modeling function, can now be used with the Unified Classification/Regression function

  • This unlocks local explainability when using Multi-task MLP models, the predictions providing reason codes with Shapley explanations (using parameters BACKGROUND_SIZE, BACKGROUND_SAMPLING_SEED)
  • However in the current update, the models are constrained a single target/label only when used via Unified Classification/Regression

 

AutoML for time series optimization enhancements

AutoML introduces an improved hyperparameter optimization for time series models

  • random-search optimization is now utilizing Hyperband
    • A new parameter WITH_HYPERBAND enables the enhancement in conjunction with SEARCH_METHOD set as "random"
    • The optimization now more efficiently allocates resources to different hyperparameter configurations, and significantly speeds up the optimization process

 

Python ML client (hana-ml) enhancements

The full list of new methods and enhancements with hana_ml 2.24  is summarized in the changelog for hana-ml 2.24 as part of the documentation. The key enhancements in this release include

Dataframe enhancements

  • New Vector- / vector-index management methods
  • New sort by vector-similarity

AutoML and pipeline modeling improvements

  • Data-parallel/massive AutoML
    progress monitor enhanced
  • New AutoML regression
    outlier detection

Text processing enhancements

  • New Text Analysis, POS, NER, Sentiment Analysis
  • Enhanced BM25 search by doc, sentence

Further misc. enhancements

  • New Tree Debriefing to text for decision tree
  • Enhanced CAP generation for AdditiveModelForecast, AutoML
    and Unified APIs wrt use of input/output signatures

You can find an examples notebook illustrating the highlighted feature enhancements here 25QRC01_2.24.ipynb. 

 

Generative AI-toolkit for SAP HANA Cloud (hana-ai)

Introduction

The new generative AI-toolkit for SAP HANA Cloud (hana-ai) is an extension of the existing Python ML client for SAP HANA (hana-ml), mainly focusing on generative AI-assisted machine learning scenario development using hana-ml, thus streamlining embedding of ML capabilities with SAP BTP Cloud Application Programming (CAP) apps.

It builds upon many leading-edge generative AI related open source Python libraries (e.g. langchain) and provides seamless integration with SAP HANA Cloud, HANA vector engine, and others Python libraries like SAP GenAI Hub SDK. 

Key capabilities for AI-assisted HANA ML development and code generation

  • Use code-template knowledge stores and new hana-ml tools
    • Tools for code generation as well as executable tools for agentic-use of HANA ML for Forecasting
  • Conversational agent for HANA ML
    • Generative AI agent, utilizing executable tools targeted for HANA ML
    • Unlocking agentic, AI-assisted HANA ML development
  • Further conversational interfaces and agents
    • HANA dataframe agent for invoke code template-based tasks using hana-ml in python
    • SQL agent for invoking code template-based and langchain-db tool tasks via SQL generation and execution
    • Conversational SmartDataFrame interface, for applying standard dataframe methods as well as code template-based tasks to a dataframe

Highlighted features

Using sample code knowledge stores

  • Leverage hana-ml samples as knowledge store
    • Python | SQL default samples for PAL functions
    • Can be augmented with additional content, created with custom / best practice templates

 

Using new set of agent-tools targeted for simplified use of HANA ML

  • Library of executable hana-ml tools
    • Tools execute independently and generate results, beyond code generation
    • Initial set of tools focused on Time Series Forecasting
    • Time series analysis tools applied to the time series data,
      determining guidance about which time series algorithms to apply
    • Can be augmented with additional, custom created tools
    • Utilized for automated and agentic-, generative AI-assisted
      HANA ML development or via direct tool-usage

 

Using new set of agent-tools targeted for simplified use of HANA ML

  • Library of executable hana-ml tools
    • Tools execute independently and generate results, beyond code generation

Using a conversational agent for HANA ML

  • New Agent for HANA ML
    • Ready to use agent to build HANA Cloud PAL forecast models
    • Conversational agent session incl. chat history, based on langchain
    • Leverages executable tools specifically targeted for HANA ML
    • HANA ML-tools: initially focused on Time Series Forecasting like
      automatic time series data analysis, forecast algorithm proposal
      and fitting, or CAP project artifact generation

 

The new generative AI-toolkit unlocks and simplifies embedded AI SAP BTP application development with natural language assistance providing a faster getting-started experience, automated code generation based on template code samples, hana-ml tools for an conversational agent.

For further details see the Introduction at github.com/SAP/generative-ai-toolkit-for-sap-hana-cloud and the documentation at sap.github.io/generative-ai-toolkit-for-sap-hana-cloud.

A sample python notebook using the conversational agent can be found at github.com/SAP-samples/hana-ml-samples/.../Generative-AI-toolkit-SAPHANACloud-Demo-SalesRefunds-Forecasting

Setup instruction for generative AI-toolkit (hana-ai)

  • Download the hana-ai package from github releases and install it using _pip install generative-ai-toolkit-for-sap-hana-cloud-x.x.x.zip_, or use _pip install hana-ai_ for install from pypi.org/hana-ai.

  • Setup of the SAP generative AI hub SDK
    • Install the required Python packages pip install "generative-ai-hub-sdk[all]".
    • Create a deployment for a generative AI model in the SAP BTP, for instructions see the SAP AI core documentation section Create a Deployment for a Generative AI Model.
    • The generative AI hub SDK reuses configuration settings from the AI-core-SDK, these include client ID, client secret, authentication URL, base URL, and resource group. You can set these values as environment variables or via a config file. For detailed instructions see this SAP Learning unit on using Generative-AI-Hub-SDK.

 Overall, again a great set of Machine Learning and AI capabilities enhancements with this SAP HANA Cloud release!

entry.title: How to identify BTP users and automate contact using PowerAutomate
entry.content:
In large SAP BTP landscapes, it’s often unclear who exactly is using a given subaccount — and more importantly, why. Especially in shared or test tenants, users might be onboarded for specific projects or evaluations, but without regular checks, it's easy to lose oversight.

In this blog post, I want to share a simple yet effective approach to:

  • extract a list of all users from a BTP subaccount,
  • enrich the data with organizational info via Microsoft PowerAutomate,
  • and automatically send personalized emails asking for the purpose of their BTP usage.

This method helped us quickly reach out to all active users and better understand current BTP utilization. It’s also lightweight and easily repeatable.


Step 1:

The first step is to download a list of all users from the relevant BTP subaccount. This can be done via the SAP BTP cockpit by navigating to Security → Users and exporting the table as an Excel file.

⚠️Important Note:

The exported Excel file might look clean at first glance — but there's a catch: all the user details are stored in a single cell, separated by commas. So, instead of having structured columns like "User Name”, “Identity Provider", etc..

To work with the data in PowerAutomate, we first need to split this combined information into separate columns.

How to split the data in Excel

  1. Open the Excel file.
  2. Select the column that contains the user data (usually Column A).
  3. Go to the Data tab in Excel.
  4. Click on Text to Columns.
  5. Choose Delimited → Click Next.
  6. Check the Comma delimiter → Click Finish.

Voilà! Now each piece of user information (email, name, origin, etc.) will be in its own column — making it much easier to work with in PowerAutomate.

Before PowerAutomate can read the file, you need to convert it into a proper Excel Table:

  1. Select all the rows and columns that contain data
  2. Go to the Insert tab in Excel
  3. Click Table
  4. In the dialog, make sure “My table has headers” is checked → Click OK
  5. Optionally, rename the table under Table Design → Table Name (e.g., BTP_Users)

Now, you need to create a new worksheet with an additional table, so that PowerAutomate can save the new data there. Make sure to define the columns with clear and meaningful headers that match the data you expect to insert later (e.g., User Name, Email, Role, etc.).

Save the file, and store it in OneDrive or SharePoint so PowerAutomate can access it.

Step 2: Enriching user data using Microsoft PowerAutomate

Now that we’ve exported and cleaned the user list from SAP BTP (see Step 1), we’ll use Microsoft PowerAutomate to enhance that list by adding organizational information such as Department and Location.

This is especially helpful for identifying which business units are using the BTP tenant — and makes the final email communication more relevant and personalized.

Step-by-step: Building the PowerAutomate Flow

Trigger: Manually start the flow

Action: “Manually trigger a flow”
→ This is how you initiate the process — useful for one-time or occasional runs.

List rows present in a table (Excel)

Action: “List rows present in a table”

Select the prepared Excel file (from Step 1) that contains the cleaned and split user data.

Apply to each user

When working with Excel rows, PowerAutomate won’t automatically create the loop to process each row. You need to add an “Apply to each” block manually.

Here’s how:

  1. After the “List rows present in a table” action, click “+ Add an action”.
  2. Search for “Apply to each” and select it.
  3. In the “Select an output from previous steps” field, choose the dynamic content labeled something like:

body/value (from “List rows present in a table”)

Your flow will now run once for each row in the Excel file.

Inside this loop, we’ll add two actions:

Inside the loop: What happens for each user?

Action 1: Get user profile (V2)

Connector: Office 365 Users, Get user profile (V2)

Input: Use the email address column from the Excel data as input.

This action fetches the full user profile from Microsoft 365 (Azure AD).

Output includes:

  1. Display name
  2. Department
  3. Office location
  4. and more

Action 2: Add a row to another Excel table

Connector: Excel Online (Business)

This action writes the enriched data into the Excel table.

You can now write values like:

  • Name and Email from the original Excel
  • Department and Location from the user profile lookup

Now that we’ve enriched our user list with department and location data in Step 2, we can use this information to send out personalized emails — one per user.

This is useful to:

  • Ask users to justify their use of the BTP tenant
  • Start cleanup or governance conversations
  • Collect feedback via a form or direct reply

If you're extending the existing flow from Step 2, this can just be the next step after enriching the Excel data.

Or you can make it a separate flow triggered manually or on a schedule.

Step-by-step: Emailing each user

  1. List rows present in a table

Action: List rows present in a table

Input: The enriched Excel file you created in Step 2

Location: OneDrive or SharePoint

Table: Make sure the table includes at least the following columns:

  • Name or First Name
  • Email
  • Department (optional)
  • Location (optional)
  1. Apply to each

Just like in Step 2, add an “Apply to each” block manually

Use value from the Excel table as the input array

Inside the loop: Send email

Action: Send an email (V2), you need to switch to advanced mode in order to use the data from the excel.

  • To: Use the Email column from your Excel row
  • Subject (example): Your usage of our SAP BTP Tenant
  • Body (example):
    Dear @{items('Apply_to_each')?['Name']},
    We noticed that you currently have access to our SAP BTP subaccount.
    In order to maintain a clean and purposeful setup, we kindly ask you to briefly explain why you are using the BTP tenant.
    Thank you! 
    Your BTP Governance Team

This approach doesn’t require any custom development and can be implemented in a few hours. While our use case was a one-time check, the setup is flexible enough to be re-used or adapted for regular audits. It also helps foster transparency and accountability in shared BTP environments.

Let me know if you have questions, or feel free to adapt this idea for your own landscape!

entry.title: The Inside SAP Series Returns – Explore How SAP’s IT Drives Transformation
entry.content:
The Inside SAP Series Returns - Explore How SAP’s IT Drives Transformation

We’re excited to announce the return of The Inside SAP series on SAP Community’s official YouTube channel, where we pull back the curtain on how SAP runs its own technology to drive innovation, enhance efficiency, and create business value. In this new season, we’ll take you inside SAP’s IT organization to show how we leverage our solutions-not just to support our business, but to transform it.

Kickoff on April 29th: SAP Runs SAP with Oliver Gutzeit

The series begins on April 29th with Oliver Gutzeit, who will take us behind the scenes of SAP runs SAP—our unique approach to using SAP technology in-house to optimize our own operations. SAP’s IT organization is at the forefront of integrating AI, data, processes, and applications to maximize efficiency and deliver business outcomes. In this session, Oliver will share how SAP’s IT team embraces digital transformation, ensuring that our workforce is equipped with the best tools to thrive.  

Whether you’re a C-level executive, IT manager, subject matter expert, or industry partner, this session will give you a firsthand look at how SAP’s IT unit drives our business transformation through innovation, experimentation, process simplification and delivering stable cutting-edge technology. 

What’s Coming Next? 

Beyond the opening session, we have even more exciting topics lined up: 

  • June 11 – How SAP Fuels Human Insights and AI Innovation with SAP Business Data Cloud 
  • June 24 – Business Data Cloud: Scale Data & AI Engineering with SAP & SAP Databricks
  • July 3 – Data Democratization Strategy Update - How SAP Attracts Citizen Users with SAP Datasphere and Collibra 

And more…the series will resume in September with more relevant and insightful content. 

Don’t miss the opportunity to hear directly from SAP’s IT leaders and gain authentic insights into how we power business transformation. Whether you’re looking to optimize your IT landscape, enhance data-driven decision-making, or explore how SAP solutions can drive innovation in your enterprise, the Inside SAP series is for you. 

Register now and be part of the conversation as we uncover how SAP’s IT team is shaping the future of business technology!

entry.title: 🚀 SAP AI Core Agent QuickLaunch Series / 秒速で学ぶ SAP AI Core Agent 開発 🚀 - Part0 Prologue / プロローグ
entry.content:
Generative AI has broken out of research labs and is now transforming the way business is done. SAP is moving at full speed to embrace this trend and has launched an agent called Joule. In this blog series, I’ll provide a “super-fast hands-on” guide to help you quickly call default models of SAP AI Core and expand them into practical AI agents for real-world business use, so you can understand how these agents work behind the scenes.

生成 AI は研究室の外へ飛び出し、今やビジネス現場の常識を塗り替えています。SAPは全速力でその波に乗っており、JouleというAgentを公開しました。このブログシリーズでは、Agentが裏側でどうなっているかを理解するため、SAP AI Core の既定モデルを最速で呼び出し、実務で使える AI Agentへ拡張する“秒速ハンズオン”をお届けします。

 

📖What You’ll Learn in This Series / 本シリーズで学べること

  • How to spin up a custom AI agent on SAP AI Core in minutes
    SAP AI Core 上でカスタム AI エージェントを “秒速” で動かす方法

  • Hands‑on with LangChain, Google Search Tool, RAG, and Streamlit
    LangChain・Google 検索ツール・RAG・Streamlit を使った実装

  • Exposing the agent as a REST API and rebuilding the UI in SAPUI5/Fiori
    エージェントを REST API 化し、UI を SAPUI5/Fiori に載せ替える手順

Time Commitment / 学習時間
Each part is designed to be completed in 10–15 minutes.
各章は 10–15 分 で読める＆手を動かせるを予定しています。

 

🗺️ Series Roadmap / 連載ロードマップ

  • Part 0 Prologue / プロローグ [current blog / 現在のブログ]
  • Part 1 Env Setup: SAP AICore & AI Launchpad / 環境構築: SAP AI CoreとAI Launchpad
  • Part 2 Building a Chat Model with LangChain / LangChain でチャットモデルを構築
  • Part 3 Agent Tools: Integrating Google Search / Agentツール: Google 検索を統合
  • Part 4 RAG Basics ①: File Upload & HANA Cloud VectorEngine / RAG 基礎 ①: ファイルアップロード & HANA Cloud ベクターエンジン
  • Part 5 RAG Basics ②: Retriever Tool / RAG 基礎 ②: Retriever Toolの構築
  • Part 6 Streamlit UI Prototype / Streamlit でチャットUI
  • Part 7 Expose as a REST API / AI Agent を REST API 化
  • Part 8 Rebuild the UI with SAPUI5 / SAPUI5 でチャットUI を再構築

Note / 注記
Subsequent blogs will be published soon.
続編は順次公開予定です。

If you enjoyed this post, please give it a kudos! Your support really motivates me. Also, if there’s anything you’d like to know more about, feel free to leave a comment!

Prologue: Fast‑Track Roadmap to SAP AI Agents
プロローグ: SAP AI エージェントへの最速ロードマップ

1 | Introduction / はじめに

The Gen‑AI wave is hitting enterprise apps—and many SAP developers wonder how to spin up their own AI agents on SAP BTP without drowning in docs. The “SAP AI QuickLaunch” series is a lightning‑fast, hands‑on guide that leverages the default‑deployed OpenAI LLM on SAP AI Core so you can launch an AI agent with minimum steps and maximum learning.

生成 AI ブームの波が業務アプリケーションにも押し寄せています。とはいえ、SAP BTP上で 自分だけの AI エージェントを動かす方法はまだ情報が少なく、「とりあえず試してみたいけど何を準備すればいいの？」と足踏みしている方も多いはず。本シリーズ 「SAP AI QuickLaunch」（秒速で学ぶ SAP AI Agent 開発） では、SAP AI Core にデフォルトでデプロイ済みの OpenAI LLM を活用し、最小ステップで AI Agentを立ち上げる超スピーディなハンズオン をお届けします。

 

2 | What You Will Build / 完成イメージ

Demo Image — Work in Progress / デモ画像は作成中

  • Streamlit Chat UI that understands uploaded files and answers your questions
    ファイルアップロードに対応し内容を理解して返答する Streamlit 製チャット UI

  • AI agent with RAG that cites both Google Search and internal docs
    Google 検索と社内ドキュメントを横断して根拠付き回答を生成する RAG エージェント

  • Future episodes will expose the agent as a REST API and rebuild the UI in SAPUI5
    後続記事ではエージェントを REST API 化し、UI を SAPUI5 に載せ替えます

 

3 | Who Should Read / 対象読者

  • SAP BTP Beginners — especially those new to AI Core
    SAP BTP 初心者 — 特に AI Core 未経験者

  • Python Developers at a basic level
    Python 初級者以上

  • Hands‑on Learners who value speed over theory
    まずは動くものが欲しい派

 

4 | Prerequisites / 事前準備

  • BTP sub-account / BTP サブアカウント
  • SAP AI Core instance / SAP AI Core インスタンス
  • SAP AI LaunchPad Subscription / SAP AI Launchpadのサブスクリプション
  • Python 3.13 and pip / Python 3.13環境 & pip
  • VSCode, BAS or any IDE / VSCodeやBASなどのIDE

 

5 | Next Up / 次回予告

Part 1 Env Setup: SAP AICore & AI Launchpad / 環境構築: SAP AI CoreとAI Launchpad

In Part 1we’ll connect to SAP AI Launchpad using the service key created for the SAP AI Core instance. Then, we’ll proceed to deploy the LLM for running a chat model — no external OpenAI key required. See you soon!

第１章では、SAP AI Core インスタンスに対して作成したサービスキーを使い、SAP AI Launchpad に接続します。その後、チャット用 LLM を動かすためのデプロイメントを実施します。外部の OpenAI API キーは不要です、お楽しみに！

 

Disclaimer / 免責事項

Disclaimer – All the views and opinions in the blog are my own and is made in my personal capacity and that SAP shall not be responsible or liable for any of the contents published in this blog.
免責事項 – 本ブログに記載された見解および意見はすべて私個人のものであり、私の個人的な立場で発信しています。SAP は本ブログの内容について一切の責任を負いません。

entry.title: 🚀 SAP AI Core Agent QuickLaunch Series / 秒速で学ぶ SAP AI Core Agent 開発 🚀 - Part 1 Env Setup / 環境構築
entry.content:
Generative AI has broken out of research labs and is now transforming the way business is done. SAP is moving at full speed to embrace this trend and has launched an agent called Joule. In this blog series, I’ll provide a “super-fast hands-on” guide to help you quickly call default models of SAP AI Core and expand them into practical AI agents for real-world business use, so you can understand how these agents work behind the scenes.

生成 AI は研究室の外へ飛び出し、今やビジネス現場の常識を塗り替えています。SAPは全速力でその波に乗っており、JouleというAgentを公開しました。このブログシリーズでは、Agentが裏側でどうなっているかを理解するため、SAP AI Core の既定モデルを最速で呼び出し、実務で使える AI Agentへ拡張する“秒速ハンズオン”をお届けします。

 

📖What You’ll Learn in This Series / 本シリーズで学べること

  • How to spin up a custom AI agent on SAP AI Core in minutes
    SAP AI Core 上でカスタム AI エージェントを “秒速” で動かす方法

  • Hands‑on with LangChain, Google Search Tool, RAG, and Streamlit
    LangChain・Google 検索ツール・RAG・Streamlit を使った実装

  • Exposing the agent as a REST API and rebuilding the UI in SAPUI5/Fiori
    エージェントを REST API 化し、UI を SAPUI5/Fiori に載せ替える手順

Time Commitment / 学習時間
Each part is designed to be completed in 10–15 minutes.
各章は 10–15 分 で読める＆手を動かせるを予定しています。

 

🗺️ Series Roadmap / 連載ロードマップ

  • Part 0 Prologue / プロローグ 
  • Part 1 Env Setup: SAP AICore & AI Launchpad / 環境構築: SAP AI CoreとAI Launchpad [current blog / 現在のブログ]
  • Part 2 Building a Chat Model with LangChain / LangChain でチャットモデルを構築
  • Part 3 Agent Tools: Integrating Google Search / Agentツール: Google 検索を統合
  • Part 4 RAG Basics ①: File Upload & HANA Cloud VectorEngine / RAG 基礎 ①: ファイルアップロード & HANA Cloud ベクターエンジン
  • Part 5 RAG Basics ②: Retriever Tool / RAG 基礎 ②: Retriever Toolの構築
  • Part 6 Streamlit UI Prototype / Streamlit でチャットUI
  • Part 7 Expose as a REST API / AI Agent を REST API 化
  • Part 8 Rebuild the UI with SAPUI5 / SAPUI5 でチャットUI を再構築

Note / 注記
Subsequent blogs will be published soon.
続編は順次公開予定です。

If you enjoyed this post, please give it a kudos! Your support really motivates me. Also, if there’s anything you’d like to know more about, feel free to leave a comment!

Env Setup: SAP AICore & AI Launchpad
環境構築: SAP AI CoreとAI Launchpad

1 | Overview / 概要
